text: Handling deadlines can be stressful, but good time management helps. Start by breaking large tasks into smaller steps with clear deadlines. Prioritize the most important tasks, focusing on what must be done first. Also, learn to manage the scope of your work—if too many tasks appear, ask if some can be delayed or removed. Finally, communicate openly with your team or supervisor. If you see a deadline slipping, inform them early. By planning, prioritizing, and staying honest about your limits, you can meet deadlines while reducing anxiety and delivering quality work.
